title: Docs Workflow Fundamentals
description: Checks understanding of why the Control Panel docs are moving from README to MkDocs.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of reorganizing the Control Panel README into a MkDocs project?
options: It automatically converts Python scripts into binaries
It creates a navigable doc site with search, themes, and permalinks
It reduces repository size by deleting historical READMEs
It forces every contributor to learn reStructuredText
questions.explanation: MkDocs converts Markdown content into a structured site with navigation, search, and theming so operators can find procedures faster.

questions.question: Which audience segmentation is recommended before rewriting the docs?
options: Admin persona, Scientist persona, and Infrastructure operators
questions.explanation: The documentation calls out three groups—admins, scientists, and infrastructure staff—so each sees the cockpit details relevant to them.

questions.question: Where should supporting assets like diagrams or screenshots live in the new structure?
options: In `docs/assets/` so MkDocs can bundle them
In the `.github` folder to avoid being published
questions.explanation: Placing media inside `docs/assets/` keeps everything versioned alongside the Markdown and ensures MkDocs can copy them into the final site.
